name: ClinProg-Bench
description: The first public benchmark for evaluating LLM and agentic systems on clinical-trial statistical programming tasks. 250 expert-curated tasks across 5 categories (Code Generation, Code Review, Spec Interpretation, Documentation, Debugging), grounded in the CDISC Pilot01 public dataset, with deterministic scoring and a language-agnostic evaluation framework.
rai:dataCollection: Tasks curated by expert statistical programmers from publicly available CDISC Pilot01 project materials. No crowdsourcing or web scraping involved. All 250 tasks authored de novo referencing public specification documents.
rai:dataCollectionMissing: None. All task fields are complete.
rai:dataBiasesDiscussion: The benchmark is grounded in a single clinical trial (CDISC Pilot01), limiting diversity of therapeutic areas and trial designs. All patient data is synthetic; no demographic biases in the evaluation substrate.
rai:dataUseCases: Evaluating LLM and agentic systems on clinical-trial statistical programming tasks. Not intended for direct clinical use or patient-level decisions.
rai:dataLimitations: Single-trial substrate; English-language prompts only; gold standards reflect one expert interpretation. See Limitations section of the paper.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The CDISC Pilot01 dataset contains only synthetic patient data.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Enables benchmarking of AI systems for GxP-regulated clinical programming, supporting safer automation in drug development.
rai:dataMaintenancePlan: Hosted on GitHub with Zenodo DOI for long-term persistence. Versioned releases planned (v0.1.x bug fixes, v0.2.0 task expansion).
citation: Anonymous. (2026). ClinProg-Bench: A Public Benchmark for Clinical Trial Statistical Programming Automation (0.1.0). Zenodo. DOI withheld for double-blind review.

distribution.name: zenodo-archive
distribution.description: Versioned Zenodo snapshot of ClinProg-Bench v0.1.0

recordSet.name: tasks
recordSet.description: 250 benchmark tasks (TaskSpec JSON files) distributed across 5 categories. Each record corresponds to one file under tasks/T{1-5}_*/.

field.name: task_id
field.description: Unique dotted task identifier, e.g. T1.1.adsl.001

field.name: category
field.description: Top-level category: T1 | T2 | T3 | T4 | T5

field.name: subcategory
field.description: Sub-category, e.g. T1.1 (SDTM ADSL), T2.1 (Log Analysis)

field.name: inputs/prompt
field.description: Natural-language task prompt delivered to the agent

field.name: expected_outputs/kind
field.description: Output modality: sas_program | r_program | python_program | json | patch | text

field.name: scoring/scorer
field.description: Scorer module: codegen | log_review | spec_extract | doc | debug

recordSet.name: gold
recordSet.description: Gold-standard reference outputs, one folder per task_id under gold/. SHA-256 hashes committed in gold/manifest.json.

field.name: sha256
field.description: SHA-256 hash of the gold output file
